MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT SIMAYOF INC. HEARING SET FOR JUL-01-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SIMAYOF INC. MOTION TO STRIKE OUT PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT THAT CLAIMS FOR ATTORNEYS FEES. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CC SECTION 1717.5 PROVIDES "IF THERE IS A WRITTEN AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES SIGNED BY THE PERSON TO BE CHARGED, THE FEES PROVIDED BY THIS SECTION MAY NOT BE IMPOSED UNLESS THAT AGREEMENT CONTAINS A STATEMENT THAT THE PREVAILING PARTY IN ANY ACTION BETWEEN THE PARTIES IS ENTITLED TO THE FEES PROVIDED BY THIS SECTION." (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR #12365
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-01-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel Gary R. Bosch for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/fee paid. Proceedings not reported. (212)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-27-2009 Set For Trial On May-10-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Apr-22-10 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 501

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2009 Continued To Nov-06-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2009 Continued To Oct-02-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SIMAYOF INC. MOTION TO STRIKE out portions of plaintiffs' complaint that claims for attorneys fees. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted without leave to amend. CC section 1717.5 provides "If there is a written agreement between the parties signed by the person to be charged, the fees provided by this section may not be imposed unless that agreement contains a statement that the prevailing party in any action between the parties is entitled to the fees provided by this section." (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365
